title: Microsoft eliminating 4800 roles in Commercial and Xbox transformation
decisionQuestion: Should Microsoft proceed with eliminating approximately 4,800 roles?
situation: Microsoft is focusing its resources on key priorities within a fast-changing industry.
objectives: Keep Microsoft positioned to deliver for customers.
Focus people, investments, and energy on strategic priorities.
conditions: The changes primarily affect the Commercial and XBOX organizations.
The elimination involves about 2.1% of the global workforce.
constraints: The decision must align with the focus on customer delivery.
The changes are specifically concentrated within Commercial and XBOX.

options.description: Proceed with eliminating the 4,800 roles as announced.
options.expectedBenefit: Better positioning to deliver for customers in a fast-changing industry.
options.principalRisk: Potential disruption or morale impact from workforce reduction.

options.description: Delay the elimination and reassess the scope of the 4,800 roles.
options.expectedBenefit: Allows for a more granular review of the workforce impact.
options.principalRisk: Missing immediate opportunities to focus on key priorities.

chosenAction: Proceed with eliminating the 4,800 roles as announced.
rejectedBenefit: Allows for a more granular review of the workforce impact.
observations: Around 4,800 roles, or 2.1% of the global workforce, are being eliminated.
The changes mostly fall within the Commercial and XBOX organizations.
inferences: The elimination is a strategic move to align the workforce with current business needs.
The decision implies that the current structure is not optimally positioned for future customer delivery.

evidenceSpans.exactQuote: Today we are eliminating around 4,800 roles, about 2.1% of our global workforce, as we focus our people, investments, and energy on the priorities that will keep Microsoft positioned to deliver for customers in a fast-changing industry.
Today’s changes mostly fall within our Commercial and XBOX organizations.
